items.question: What penalty does Ridge regression add to the least-squares loss?
items.explanation: Ridge uses the L2 (squared) penalty on coefficients: λ||β||₂² = λ Σ βⱼ².

items.question: Which method is most associated with sparse coefficient vectors?
items.explanation: L1 penalties have corners on axes, so optima often land with some βⱼ exactly zero.

items.question: What is the Ridge closed-form solution (with invertible XᵀX + λI)?
items.explanation: Ridge adds λI to the normal equations, stabilizing inversion and shrinking β.

items.question: Before fitting Lasso on mixed-scale numeric features, you should:
items.explanation: Scaling puts features on comparable units so the L1 penalty treats each coefficient fairly.
